page: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge is a dense-storage instance type designed for workloads requiring high local storage capacity. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor running at 2.4 GHz. The instance provides 8 virtual CPUs (4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled), 61.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. A key hardware characteristic is the inclusion of 12,288 GB of local HDD storage, while no dedicated GPU resources are present.

Benchmark data highlights a clear tradeoff between massive storage capacity and compute performance. The Intel Xeon processor delivers weak to poor CPU performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 740.69 and a multi-core score of 4142.49. Memory performance is mixed, with a weak write bandwidth of 18.26 GB/sec on large blocks but an average read-only large block bandwidth of 39.96 GB/sec. Database operations on Passmark score in the average tier at 2482.18, whereas Redis SET operations are weak at 2.47 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d2.2xlarge offers a high density of local HDD storage per unit of compute, making it a specialized option where storage volume is prioritized over raw processing speed. Due to the weak single-core and multi-core CPU metrics, it is poorly suited for compute-intensive tasks or modern LLM inference. This instance is best utilized for storage-heavy workloads such as MapReduce logging, data warehousing, large-scale Hadoop deployments, and legacy database systems requiring massive local sequential disk access.
description: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge is a dense-storage instance built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor with 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB of memory, and 12,288 GB of local HDD storage. Operating on the Xen hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant tradeoff, with weak CPU and memory write speeds but average large-block memory read bandwidth and database operation scores. Compute-heavy tasks and LLM inference perform poorly on this hardware. Consequently, the d2.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-dense applications rather than compute-bound workloads. It is best suited for data warehousing, MapReduce, and large-scale sequential storage applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge is a dense-storage instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB of RAM, and 12,288 GB of local HDD storage, designed for sequential data warehousing and MapReduce workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge features 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB RAM, and 12,288 GB of local HDD storage, optimized for dense storage workloads.
tagline: A dense-storage instance pairing 12 TB of local HDD capacity with moderate compute resources for sequential data processing workloads.
bullet_points: Features 12,288 GB of local HDD storage for dense data capacity.
Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor.
Equipped with 61.0 GB of system memory and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers average performance in large-block memory reads and database operations.
Exhibits weak single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark results.
Best suited for MapReduce, data warehousing, and large sequential log processing.
